content: Just as the human learning schema in high school language arts frames 'reflexion' as both a literal reflection of external input and a deliberate metacognitive practice of revising one’s own thinking, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to integrate vocabulary acquisition-style scaffolding to ground abstract self-correction. Specifically, structured vocabulary mapping exercises used in literacy education can be adapted to help LLMs explicitly anchor inconsistent problem-solving perspectives to concrete semantic concepts, reducing ungrounded self-criticism and improving reflection accuracy.
